rule: Damage roll math should have one canonical authority.
currentEvidence: Both scripts/combat/rolls/damage.js and scripts/combat/rolls/attacks.js export rollDamage implementations with different option handling.

rule: Damage buttons must preserve attack option and target outcome context.
currentEvidence: holo-roll damage buttons store actorId, weaponId, isCritical, critMultiplier, and twoHanded only.

rule: Area attacks do not deal double damage on a critical hit.
currentEvidence: damage.js skips critical multiplication if area context survives, but chat damage buttons do not preserve areaAttack/autofire context.

rule: Evasion halves area damage on hit and negates area damage on miss.
currentEvidence: Evasion metadata exists, but legacy autofire helper appears to inspect attacker items; canonical damage path lacks area hit/miss target context.
automationBoundary: automate after GM selects affected targets/outcome

rule: Burst Fire is a single-target attack and should not deal half damage on a miss.
currentEvidence: Legacy enhanced-rolls Burst Fire branch appears to label misses as half damage.

rule: Burst Fire adds +2 weapon dice and consumes five shots when selected.
currentEvidence: Burst Fire metadata has +2 dice and ammo cost, but chat damage buttons do not preserve the selected burstFire attack option context.

rule: Successful stun damage subtracts half damage from HP, compares original stun damage to DT, and moves -2 CT if it meets/exceeds DT.
currentEvidence: DamageResolutionEngine treats damageType stun as a threshold flag, but stun special threshold behavior is gated by a disabled-by-default houserule and half-HP loss is not clearly applied.

rule: Only creatures can be stunned; droids, vehicles, and objects are immune to stun damage.
currentEvidence: Droid traits mention immunity, but DamageResolutionEngine/DamageMitigationManager do not clearly reject stun damage for droids, vehicles, or objects.

rule: A hit always deals at least 1 point of damage even if penalties reduce damage below 1.
currentEvidence: No clear floor-to-one enforcement was found in the primary damage formula or result handling.

rule: Shooting or throwing into melee applies -5 unless suppressed by Precise Shot.
currentEvidence: Precise Shot suppresses firingIntoMeleePenalty in metadata, but the attack dialog does not expose a clear Firing into melee checkbox and the base penalty is not reliably applied.

rule: Elusive Target adds an additional -5 ranged attack penalty when the defender is fighting in melee, stacking with firing into melee.
currentEvidence: Talent metadata already marks Elusive Target as manual; user confirmed this should remain GM adjudicated because map/engagement detection is too hard and too table-dependent.

rule: Mixed damage types should allow abilities keyed to one component to affect the whole simultaneous damage effect.
currentEvidence: Some context builders support damageTypes arrays, but damage chat and mitigation paths often collapse to a single damageType string.
